creator: jwagner@graffadmin.com
text:
Thanks for your reply Eric.  I'm about ready to dump amavisd-new myself, 
but since I don't use SpamAssassin, have no good alternative.  If I turn 
off amavisd, there will then be no virus scanning of incoming messages, 
which is something I don't want to have happen.  No mail was delivered 
to local users all weekend again and people were screaming when I got to 
work today.  It is somehow comforting to know that I'm not the only one 
with this problem though - initially I was afraid I'd done something to 
screw it up.  I have noticed a couple of interesting things that may or 
may not aid in isolating the problem:

Mail can be sent to people at other machines using SMTP on this machine; 
it's only mail destined for local users that's affected.  Like Eric, it 
seems to be only certain messages, but once a message gets stuck, it 
causes a build-up of ALL messages to be not delivered.  Once I kill 
amavisd and restart it, I can run postqueue -f and mail starts being 
delivered.  Last weekend, there were 6 messages that refused to be 
delivered no matter how many times I killed and restarted amavisd-new.  
 From last Thursday until now, there were 10.

Even though I down-graded to a previous version of amavisd-new that did 
not have this problem when I used it in the past (the tarball was still 
in /usr/ports/distfiles), the trouble is still occurring.  To downgrade, 
I used make deinstall in the new port directory to remove 2.6.4, then 
copied the old port directory over the new one, then make, make 
reinstall for the older port.  All that I can think of is that 
installing the new version modified something that wasn't removed on 
deinstall and is also causing the same symptoms with the old version of 
the port.



I did find a hackish workaround for this problem.  I created a simple 
shell script (see below) to kill amavisd-new and restart it, then try to 
flush the mail queue.  I set it to run every hour from cron.  While it 
doesn't help at all with the stuck messages, it should prevent the 
massive build-up whenever this problem occurs that I've been 
experiencing since this began.  I still have to manually copy & paste 
the stuck messages into a new message to the recipient and delete the 
original message from the queue manually.  Hopefully a fix will be found 
soon.

Gabor, if there's anything you need from me to aid in troubleshooting, 
please feel free to drop me an e-mail and I'll do my best to help.  
While I'm no means an expert at this stuff, I know my way around a unix 
box.  From troubleshooting my own programs, I realize that it's almost 
impossible to troubleshoot a problem if you can't reproduce it yourself.

Jim


#!/bin/csh
/usr/local/etc/rc.d/amavisd stop
/bin/sleep 3
/usr/bin/killall amavisd
/bin/sleep 3
/usr/local/etc/rc.d/amavisd start
/bin/sleep 3
/usr/local/sbin/postqueue -f
